evals.prompt: I have a REST API at https://quickpizza.grafana.com that I need to load test. I want to simulate 50 concurrent users hitting the /api/json endpoint for 5 minutes and make sure the 95th percentile response time stays under 500ms and error rate is below 1%. Write me a k6 script.
evals.expected_output: A k6 script with ramping-vus or constant-vus executor targeting 50 VUs for 5 minutes with appropriate thresholds for p(95)<500 and error rate<0.01, with checks on response status
expectations: Script imports http from 'k6/http'
Script sets thresholds with http_req_duration p(95)<500
Script sets thresholds with http_req_failed rate<0.01
Script targets approximately 50 VUs
Script runs for approximately 5 minutes
Script includes check() calls to validate responses
Script includes sleep() between requests for realistic pacing
Script hits the /api/json endpoint

evals.prompt: I need to find out how many requests per second my checkout API can handle before it breaks. The endpoint is POST https://quickpizza.grafana.com/api/json with a JSON body {"name": "test"}. Write a k6 breakpoint test that gradually increases load until the system fails. Auto-stop the test if error rate goes above 5% or response time exceeds 3 seconds.
evals.expected_output: A k6 breakpoint test using ramping-arrival-rate executor with gradually increasing load and abortOnFail thresholds
expectations: Script uses ramping-arrival-rate executor
Script sends POST requests with JSON body containing 'name' field
Script sets Content-Type header to application/json
Thresholds use abortOnFail: true
Script has a threshold for error rate above 5%
Script has a threshold for response time p(95) or p(99) < 3000ms
Script gradually ramps up the request rate over time
Script sets preAllocatedVUs and maxVUs

evals.prompt: Write me a k6 load test that simulates a realistic user journey on an e-commerce site at https://quickpizza.grafana.com. The journey should be: 1) browse homepage, 2) view a product at /api/json, 3) add to cart via POST /api/json. I want 3 phases: ramp up to 30 users over 2 min, hold for 5 min, then ramp down over 1 min. Track custom metrics for each step and set thresholds per step.
evals.expected_output: A k6 script with groups for each step, custom metrics per step, ramping-vus stages matching the described phases, and per-step thresholds using tags
expectations: Script uses group() to organize the user journey steps
Script has ramp-up, hold, and ramp-down stages matching the spec
Script includes custom metrics (Trend, Counter, or Rate) for individual steps
Script sets thresholds that target specific steps using tags or group names
Script includes check() for each request
Script includes realistic sleep() between steps
